req.title: Patient portal login (audit)
req.statement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be rate-limited and notify the patient. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# pytest Tests for SSO Login, Rate Limiting, and AuditEvent Requirements

tests/

## conftest.py

```python
"""
Shared fixtures and configuration for SSO, rate-limiting, and audit event tests.
"""

import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest
import pytest_asyncio
from httpx import AsyncClient

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

BASE_URL = "http://testserver"
SSO_CALLBACK_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/auth/sso/callback"
SSO_LOGIN_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/auth/sso/login"
AUDIT_EVENTS_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/audit/events"
RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5
RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS = 300  # 5 minutes
LOCKOUT_DURATION_SECONDS = 900   # 15 minutes


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Patient fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def valid_patient():
    """A fully provisioned patient identity."""
    return {
        "patient_id": "patient-001",
        "email": "alice@example.com",
        "phone": "+15550001111",
        "sso_subject": "auth0|abc123",
        "display_name": "Alice Patient",
        "notification_preferences": {"email": True, "sms": True},
    }


@pytest.fixture
def locked_out_patient():
    """A patient whose account is currently locked after repeated failures."""
    return {
        "patient_id": "patient-999",
        "email": "locked@example.com",
        "phone": "+15559999999",
        "sso_subject": "auth0|locked999",
        "display_name": "Locked Patient",
        "failed_attempts": RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS,
        "lockout_until": datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp() + LOCKOUT_DURATION_SECONDS,
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SSO token fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def valid_id_token():
    """Minimal valid OIDC ID-token claims."""
    return {
        "sub": "auth0|abc123",
        "iss": "https://idp.example.com/",
        "aud": "client-id-001",
        "exp": int(time.time()) + 3600,
        "iat": int(time.time()),
        "email": "alice@example.com",
        "email_verified": True,
        "nonce": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }


@pytest.fixture
def expired_id_token(valid_id_token):
    """An OIDC token whose expiry is in the past."""
    token = valid_id_token.copy()
    token["exp"] = int(time.time()) - 60
    return token


@pytest.fixture
def tampered_id_token(valid_id_token):
    """An OIDC token with a mismatched audience (simulates tampering)."""
    token = valid_id_token.copy()
    token["aud"] = "wrong-client-id"
    return token


@pytest.fixture
def unverified_email_token(valid_id_token):
    """Token for a patient whose email has not been verified at the IdP."""
    token = valid_id_token.copy()
    token["email_verified"] = False
    return token


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Audit event fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def audit_event_schema():
    """Required top-level keys for a conformant AuditEvent."""
    return {
        "required_keys": [
            "event_id",
            "event_type",
            "actor_id",
            "actor_type",
            "resource_type",
            "resource_id",
            "action",
            "outcome",
            "timestamp",
            "ip_address",
            "user_agent",
            "correlation_id",
        ],
        "valid_outcomes": ["success", "failure", "error"],
        "valid_event_types": [
            "SSO_LOGIN_INITIATED",
            "SSO_LOGIN_SUCCESS",
            "SSO_LOGIN_FAILURE",
            "SSO_LOGIN_BLOCKED",
            "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
            "NOTIFICATION_SENT",
            "ACCOUNT_LOCKED",
            "ACCOUNT_UNLOCKED",
        ],
    }


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_store():
    """In-memory list acting as the audit-event sink."""
    store: list[dict] = []
    return store


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Application / HTTP client fixture
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest_asyncio.fixture
async def async_client(mock_audit_store):
    """
    Async HTTP test client wired to the application.
    Swaps the real audit sink with an in-memory store for inspection.
    """
    # Import here so the module is only required when tests run
    from app.main import create_app  # noqa: PLC0415 (project-specific import)

    app = create_app()

    # Inject the mock audit store so tests can assert on emitted events
    app.state.audit_store = mock_audit_store

    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url=BASE_URL) as client:
        yield client, mock_audit_store


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Notification service mock
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def mock_notification_service():
    """Captures notifications sent during tests."""
    with patch("app.services.notifications.NotificationService.send") as mock_send:
        mock_send.return_value = AsyncMock(return_value={"status": "delivered"})
        yield mock_send


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Redis / rate-limit store mock
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def mock_rate_limit_store():
    """Simulates a Redis-backed rate-limit counter."""
    store: dict[str, int] = {}

    def increment(key: str, window: int = RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS) -> int:
        store[key] = store.get(key, 0) + 1
        return store[key]

    def get_count(key: str) -> int:
        return store.get(key, 0)

    def reset(key: str) -> None:
        store.pop(key, None)

    mock = MagicMock()
    mock.increment = increment
    mock.get_count = get_count
    mock.reset = reset
    mock.store = store
    return mock
```

## test_sso_login.py

```python
"""
Tests for SSO login flows:
  - Successful login
  - Token validation failures (expired, tampered, unverified email)
  - Account not found / provisioning edge cases
  - Logout / session invalidation
"""

import pytest
import pytest_asyncio
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _assert_audit_event(
    audit_store: list,
    expected_event_type: str,
    expected_outcome: str,
    patient_id: str | None = None,
) -> dict:
    """
    Locate and return an audit event matching the given criteria.
    Raises AssertionError with a descriptive message if not found.
    """
    matches = [
        e for e in audit_store
        if e.get("event_type") == expected_event_type
        and e.get("outcome") == expected_outcome
        and (patient_id is None or e.get("actor_id") == patient_id)
    ]
    assert matches, (
        f"No AuditEvent found: type={expected_event_type!r} "
        f"outcome={expected_outcome!r} actor_id={patient_id!r}\n"
        f"Stored events: {audit_store}"
    )
    return matches[-1]  # return the most recent matching event


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SSO_LOGIN_INITIATED tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestSSOLoginInitiation:
    """Verify that navigating to the login page triggers the correct audit event."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_login_initiation_emits_audit_event(self, async_client):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        response = await client.get(
            "/auth/sso/login",
            headers={"X-Forwarded-For": "10.0.0.1", "User-Agent": "TestBrowser/1.0"},
        )

        assert response.status_code in (200, 302), (
            f"Expected redirect or OK, got {response.status_code}"
        )
        event = _assert_audit_event(
            audit_store, "SSO_LOGIN_INITIATED", "success"
        )
        assert event["correlation_id"], "correlation_id must be populated"
        assert event["ip_address"] == "10.0.0.1"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_login_initiation_includes_redirect_uri(self, async_client):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        response = await client.get(
            "/auth/sso/login",
            params={"redirect_uri": "https://portal.example.com/dashboard"},
        )

        # Should redirect to the IdP
        assert response.status_code == 302
        assert "idp.example.com" in response.headers.get("location", ""), (
            "Login should redirect to the configured IdP"
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SSO_LOGIN_SUCCESS tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestSSOLoginSuccess:
    """Successful SSO callback scenarios."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_successful_login_returns_session(
        self, async_client, valid_patient, valid_id_token
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_id_token),
        ), patch(
            "app.repositories.patient.PatientRepository.get_by_sso_subject",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_patient),
        ):
            response = await client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"code": "valid-auth-code", "state": "secure-state-123"},
                headers={"X-Forwarded-For": "10.0.0.1"},
            )

        assert response.status_code == 200
        body = response.json()
        assert "session_token" in body, "Response must contain a session token"
        assert body["patient_id"] == valid_patient["patient_id"]

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_successful_login_emits_success_audit_event(
        self, async_client, valid_patient, valid_id_token
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_id_token),
        ), patch(
            "app.repositories.patient.PatientRepository.get_by_sso_subject",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_patient),
        ):
            await client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"code": "valid-auth-code", "state": "secure-state-123"},
            )

        event = _assert_audit_event(
            audit_store,
            "SSO_LOGIN_SUCCESS",
            "success",
            patient_id=valid_patient["patient_id"],
        )
        assert event["resource_type"] == "Patient"
        assert event["resource_id"] == valid_patient["patient_id"]
        assert event["action"] == "LOGIN"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_successful_login_resets_failed_attempt_counter(
        self, async_client, valid_patient, valid_id_token, mock_rate_limit_store
    ):
        """A successful login clears any previously accumulated failure count."""
        client, audit_store = async_client

        # Pre-seed some failures below the lockout threshold
        key = f"sso_failures:{valid_patient['patient_id']}"
        for _ in range(3):
            mock_rate_limit_store.increment(key)

        assert mock_rate_limit_store.get_count(key) == 3

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_id_token),
        ), patch(
            "app.repositories.patient.PatientRepository.get_by_sso_subject",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_patient),
        ), patch(
            "app.services.rate_limit.RateLimitService.store",
            mock_rate_limit_store,
        ):
            response = await client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"code": "valid-auth-code", "state": "secure-state-123"},
            )

        assert response.status_code == 200
        assert mock_rate_limit_store.get_count(key) == 0, (
            "Failure counter must be reset after a successful login"
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SSO_LOGIN_FAILURE tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestSSOLoginFailure:
    """Failed callback scenarios — expired tokens, tampered tokens, etc."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_expired_token_returns_401(
        self, async_client, expired_id_token
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("Token has expired")),
        ):
            response = await client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"code": "expired-code", "state": "secure-state-123"},
                headers={"X-Forwarded-For": "10.0.0.2"},
            )

        assert response.status_code == 401
        body = response.json()
        assert "error" in body
        assert body["error"] == "authentication_failed"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_expired_token_emits_failure_audit_event(
        self, async_client, expired_id_token
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("Token has expired")),
        ):
            await client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"code": "expired-code", "state": "secure-state-123"},
                headers={"X-Forwarded-For": "10.0.0.2"},
            )

        event = _assert_audit_event(audit_store, "SSO_LOGIN_FAILURE", "failure")
        assert "Token has expired" in event.get("failure_reason", ""), (
            "AuditEvent must record the failure reason"
        )
        assert event["ip_address"] == "10.0.0.2"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_tampered_token_returns_401(self, async_client, tampered_id_token):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("Invalid audience")),
        ):
            response = await client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"code": "tampered-code", "state": "secure-state-123"},
            )

        assert response.status_code == 401

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_tampered_token_emits_failure_audit_event(
        self, async_client, tampered_id_token
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("Invalid audience")),
        ):
            await client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"code": "tampered-code", "state": "secure-state-123"},
            )

        _assert_audit_event(audit_store, "SSO_LOGIN_FAILURE", "failure")

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_unverified_email_returns_403(
        self, async_client, unverified_email_token, valid_patient
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=unverified_email_token),
        ), patch(
            "app.repositories.patient.PatientRepository.get_by_sso_subject",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_patient),
        ):
            response = await client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"code": "unverified-code", "state": "secure-state-123"},
            )

        assert response.status_code == 403
        assert response.json()["error"] == "email_not_verified"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_unknown_patient_returns_404(self, async_client, valid_id_token):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_id_token),
        ), patch(
            "app.repositories.patient.PatientRepository.get_by_sso_subject",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=None),
        ):
            response = await client.post(
                "/auth/sso/callback",
                json={"code": "ghost-code", "state": "secure-state-123"},
            )

        assert response.status_code == 404

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_missing_code_parameter_returns_400(self, async_client):
        client, _ = async_client

        response = await client.post(
            "/auth/sso/callback",
            json={"state": "secure-state-123"},  # "code" intentionally omitted
        )

        assert response.status_code == 400

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_missing_state_parameter_returns_400(self, async_client):
        client, _ = async_client

        response = await client.post(
            "/auth/sso/callback",
            json={"code": "some-code"},  # "state" intentionally omitted
        )

        assert response.status_code == 400
```

## test_rate_limiting.py

```python
"""
Tests for SSO failure rate-limiting:
  - Counter increments on each failure
  - Account lockout after threshold
  - Lockout notification sent to the patient
  - Locked accounts are blocked with 429
  - Lockout expiry restores access
  - AuditEvent emitted at every relevant step
"""

import time
import pytest
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

from tests.conftest import RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS, LOCKOUT_DURATION_SECONDS


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_failure_request(client, attempt_number: int = 1):
    """Simulate one failed SSO callback (bad token)."""
    return client.post(
        "/auth/sso/callback",
        json={
            "code": f"bad-code-{attempt_number}",
            "state": "secure-state-123",
        },
        headers={"X-Forwarded-For": "10.1.0.1"},
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Counter behaviour
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestRateLimitCounter:
    """The failure counter increments correctly and resets on success."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_failure_increments_counter(
        self, async_client, mock_rate_limit_store
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("bad token")),
        ), patch(
            "app.services.rate_limit.RateLimitService.store",
            mock_rate_limit_store,
        ):
            response = await _make_failure_request(client, attempt_number=1)

        assert response.status_code == 401
        # Counter for the IP should be 1
        assert mock_rate_limit_store.get_count("sso_failures:10.1.0.1") == 1

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("attempt", range(1, RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS))
    async def test_sub_threshold_failures_return_401_not_429(
        self, async_client, mock_rate_limit_store, attempt
    ):
        """Failures below the threshold still return 401, not 429."""
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("bad token")),
        ), patch(
            "app.services.rate_limit.RateLimitService.store",
            mock_rate_limit_store,
        ):
            for n in range(attempt):
                response = await _make_failure_request(client, attempt_number=n + 1)

        assert response.status_code == 401, (
            f"Expected 401 for attempt {attempt}, got {response.status_code}"
        )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_each_failure_emits_sso_login_failure_audit_event(
        self, async_client, mock_rate_limit_store
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        failure_count = 3

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("bad token")),
        ), patch(
            "app.services.rate_limit.RateLimitService.store",
            mock_rate_limit_store,
        ):
            for n in range(failure_count):
                await _make_failure_request(client, attempt_number=n + 1)

        failure_events = [
            e for e in audit_store if e.get("event_type") == "SSO_LOGIN_FAILURE"
        ]
        assert len(failure_events) == failure_count, (
            f"Expected {failure_count} SSO_LOGIN_FAILURE events, "
            f"found {len(failure_events)}"
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Lockout behaviour
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAccountLockout:
    """After MAX_ATTEMPTS failures, the account is locked and the patient notified."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_exceeding_threshold_returns_429(
        self, async_client, mock_rate_limit_store, mock_notification_service, valid_patient
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("bad token")),
        ), patch(
            "app.repositories.patient.PatientRepository.get_by_ip_or_subject",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_patient),
        ), patch(
            "app.services.rate_limit.RateLimitService.store",
            mock_rate_limit_store,
        ):
            for n in range(RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS + 1):
                response = await _make_failure_request(client, attempt_number=n + 1)

        assert response.status_code == 429, (
            "The request after exceeding the threshold must return 429 Too Many Requests"
        )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_exceeding_threshold_emits_rate_limit_exceeded_event(
        self, async_client, mock_rate_limit_store, mock_notification_service, valid_patient
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("bad token")),
        ), patch(
            "app.repositories.patient.PatientRepository.get_by_ip_or_subject",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_patient),
        ), patch(
            "app.services.rate_limit.RateLimitService.store",
            mock_rate_limit_store,
        ):
            for n in range(RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS + 1):
                await _make_failure_request(client, attempt_number=n + 1)

        rate_limit_events = [
            e for e in audit_store if e.get("event_type") == "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
        ]
        assert len(rate_limit_events) >= 1, (
            "At least one RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED AuditEvent must be emitted"
        )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_lockout_emits_account_locked_audit_event(
        self, async_client, mock_rate_limit_store, mock_notification_service, valid_patient
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("bad token")),
        ), patch(
            "app.repositories.patient.PatientRepository.get_by_ip_or_subject",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_patient),
        ), patch(
            "app.services.rate_limit.RateLimitService.store",
            mock_rate_limit_store,
        ):
            for n in range(RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS + 1):
                await _make_failure_request(client, attempt_number=n + 1)

        locked_events = [
            e for e in audit_store if e.get("event_type") == "ACCOUNT_LOCKED"
        ]
        assert len(locked_events) >= 1, "ACCOUNT_LOCKED AuditEvent must be emitted"
        assert locked_events[0]["resource_type"] == "Patient"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_lockout_notifies_patient_via_email(
        self, async_client, mock_rate_limit_store, mock_notification_service, valid_patient
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("bad token")),
        ), patch(
            "app.repositories.patient.PatientRepository.get_by_ip_or_subject",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_patient),
        ), patch(
            "app.services.rate_limit.RateLimitService.store",
            mock_rate_limit_store,
        ):
            for n in range(RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS + 1):
                await _make_failure_request(client, attempt_number=n + 1)

        mock_notification_service.assert_called()
        call_args = mock_notification_service.call_args_list[-1]
        notification = call_args[1] if call_args[1] else call_args[0][0]

        assert notification["channel"] in ("email", "sms", "push"), (
            "Notification must use a known channel"
        )
        assert notification["recipient"] == valid_patient["email"]
        assert "locked" in notification["body"].lower() or \
               "suspicious" in notification["body"].lower(), (
            "Notification body must reference the lockout or suspicious activity"
        )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_lockout_notifies_patient_via_sms(
        self, async_client, mock_rate_limit_store, mock_notification_service, valid_patient
    ):
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("bad token")),
        ), patch(
            "app.repositories.patient.PatientRepository.get_by_ip_or_subject",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_patient),
        ), patch(
            "app.services.rate_limit.RateLimitService.store",
            mock_rate_limit_store,
        ):
            for n in range(RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS + 1):
                await _make_failure_request(client, attempt_number=n + 1)

        channels_notified = [
            call[1].get("channel", call[0][0].get("channel", ""))
            if call[1] else call[0][0].get("channel", "")
            for call in mock_notification_service.call_args_list
        ]
        assert "sms" in channels_notified, (
            "Patient with SMS preference enabled must receive an SMS notification"
        )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_notification_emits_audit_event(
        self, async_client, mock_rate_limit_store, mock_notification_service, valid_patient
    ):
        """Every outbound notification must itself be captured in an AuditEvent."""
        client, audit_store = async_client

        with patch(
            "app.services.sso.SSOService.validate_token",
            new=AsyncMock(side_effect=ValueError("bad token")),
        ), patch(
            "app.repositories.patient.PatientRepository.get_by_ip_or_subject",
            new=AsyncMock(return_value=valid_patient),
        ), patch(
            "app.services.rate_limit.RateLimitService.store",
            mock_rate_limit_store,
        ):
            for n in range(RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS + 1):
                await _make_failure_request(client, attempt_number=n + 1)

        notification_events = [
            e for e in audit_store if e.get("event_type") == "NOTIFICATION_SENT"
        ]
        assert len(notification_events) >= 1, (
            "A NOTIFICATION_SENT AuditEvent must be emitted for each notification"
        )
        assert notification_events[0]["outcome"] == "success"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Locked account access
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    @pytest.mark.asyncio

import time

from datetime import datetime, timezone
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

from httpx import AsyncClient

SSO_LOGIN_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/auth/sso/login"
AUDIT_EVENTS_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/audit/events"
RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5
RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS = 300  # 5 minutes
LOCKOUT_DURATION_SECONDS = 900   # 15 minutes


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def valid_patient():
    """A fully provisioned patient identity."""
    return {
        "patient_id": "patient-001",
        "email": "alice@example.com",
        "phone": "+15550001111",
        "sso_subject": "auth0|abc123",
        "display_name": "Alice Patient",
        "notification_preferences": {"email": True, "sms": True},
    }


@pytest.fixture
def locked_out_patient():
    """A patient whose account is currently locked after repeated failures."""
    return {
        "patient_id": "patient-999",
        "email": "locked@example.com",
        "phone": "+15559999999",
        "sso_subject": "auth0|locked999",
        "display_name": "Locked Patient",

@pytest.fixture
def valid_id_token():
    """Minimal valid OIDC ID-token claims."""
    return {
        "sub": "auth0|abc123",
        "iss": "https://idp.example.com/",
        "aud": "client-id-001",
        "exp": int(time.time()) + 3600,
        "iat": int(time.time()),
        "email": "alice@example.com",
        "email_verified": True,
        "nonce": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }


@pytest.fixture
def expired_id_token(valid_id_token):
    """An OIDC token whose expiry is in the past."""
    token = valid_id_token.copy()
    token["exp"] = int(time.time()) - 60
    return token


@pytest.fixture
def tampered_id_token(valid_id_token):
    """An OIDC token with a mismatched audience (simulates tampering)."""
    token = valid_id_token.copy()
    token["aud"] = "wrong-client-id"
    return token


@pytest.fixture
def unverified_email_token(valid_id_token):
    """Token for a patient whose email has not been verified at the IdP."""
    token = valid_id_token.copy()
    token["email_verified"] = False
    return token
